source.title: The struggle of giving up personal goals: affective, physiological, and cognitive consequences of an action crisis

confidence_rationale: The most mechanistically instrumented paper in the strand: the pre-disengagement 'action crisis' is a measurable, affectively-signed, cortisol-elevating CONFLICT state with concurrent automatic goal-devaluation. Supports the appraisal-not-evidence-tally trigger but specifically WEAKENS the one-shot framing -- the trigger is an extended contested struggle, and goal-devaluation during the crisis is a symptom, not proof of unattainability.
failure_signatures: Too late / unresolved crisis: prolonged intrapsychic conflict -> chronic cortisol elevation, performance decrement, well-being collapse (the rumination/persistence pole, physiologically instrumented).
Too early: premature crisis resolution toward disengagement -> goal devaluation and abandonment of a still-viable goal before genuine setback.
Devaluation-as-trigger error: cognitive goal-devaluation occurs DURING the crisis, so abandoning on the devaluation signal alone risks abandoning before the conflict is genuinely resolved.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Disengagement is preceded by an 'action crisis' -- a critical phase of intrapsychic conflict between continued pursuit and abandonment, triggered when setbacks make disengagement a live option; it degrades well-being, elevates cortisol, impairs later performance, and is accompanied by automatic dampening of the cognitive evaluation of the goal itself. Disengagement is a process, not a discrete event.
mapping.ree_translation: Sharpens ARC-078 C3: the abandon trigger is not a clean threshold crossing but a brief contested STATE with an affective/physiological signature and concurrent goal-devaluation. A REE abandon mechanism modelled as a costless one-shot flag discards the conflict-resolution dynamics the data show are the actual mechanism; and goal-devaluation must NOT be the abandon trigger (it is a within-crisis symptom).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The action crisis is an effortful, metabolically costly struggle (cortisol) explicitly NOT a switch; mapping it onto a costless one-step flag is a category error. Field/longitudinal self-report + physiology, healthy goal-pursuit (students, marathon runners), not clinical.
mapping.source_context: Two longitudinal field studies + a marathon-runner study; psychological well-being, salivary cortisol, subsequent race performance; personal-goal pursuit in healthy adults.

confidence_components.notes: Strong for the strand (longitudinal + physiological instrumentation of the pre-disengagement state). Mapping fidelity moderate: directly characterises the trigger STATE but in healthy goal pursuit; the one-shot-flag contradiction is a high-value design finding. Transfer risk moderate.
